exercises.starterCode:
# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.

starterFiles.content:
# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.

Generated summary

Created automatically from project notes.

hiddenShellCheck.script:
set -eu
repo=community-project
fail() { printf "%s\n" "$1" >&2; exit 1; }
git -C "$repo" rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree >/dev/null 2>&1 || fail "The expected Git repository is missing."
[ "$(git -C "$repo" branch --show-current)" = main ] || fail "The repository is not on main."
[ "$(git -C "$repo" rev-list --count HEAD 2>/dev/null || printf 0)" = 5 ] || fail "The repository has the wrong number of commits."
[ -z "$(git -C "$repo" status --porcelain)" ] || fail "The working tree is not clean yet."
git -C "$repo" check-ignore -q -- generated/summary.txt || fail 'generated/summary.txt should be ignored.'
git -C "$repo" check-ignore -q -- generated/preview.txt || fail 'generated/preview.txt should be ignored.'
git -C "$repo" check-ignore -q -- debug.log || fail 'debug.log should be ignored.'
[ -f "$repo/README.md" ] || fail 'README.md is missing.'
[ "$(cat "$repo/README.md")" = '# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.' ] || fail 'README.md does not contain the required text.'

workspace.starterCode:
# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.

starterFiles.content:
# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.

Generated summary

Created automatically from project notes.

fixtureFiles.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- community-project
mkdir -p -- community-project
git -C community-project init -q -b main
git -C community-project config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'
git -C community-project config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 10:00 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0100
' > community-project/contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Old planning notes

Spring flyer draft.
' > community-project/old-notes.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project/generated
printf %s 'Generated summary

Created automatically from project notes.
' > community-project/generated/summary.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Start community project'
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 9:30 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Update community schedule'
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0130
' > community-project/contacts.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Update community contacts'
rm -f -- community-project/schedule.txt
rm -f -- community-project/old-notes.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 9:30 AM
' > community-project/events.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-04T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-04T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Organize community project files'
rm -f -- community-project/generated/summary.txt
rmdir -- community-project/generated 2>/dev/null || true
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'generated/
*.log
' > community-project/.gitignore
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-05T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-05T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Ignore generated output'
mkdir -p -- community-project/generated
printf %s 'Generated summary

Created automatically from project notes.
' > community-project/generated/summary.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project/generated
printf %s '' > community-project/generated/preview.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '' > community-project/debug.log

generated/
*.log

hiddenShellCheck.script:
set -eu
repo=community-project
fail() { printf "%s\n" "$1" >&2; exit 1; }
git -C "$repo" rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree >/dev/null 2>&1 || fail "The expected Git repository is missing."
[ "$(git -C "$repo" branch --show-current)" = main ] || fail "The repository is not on main."
[ "$(git -C "$repo" rev-list --count HEAD 2>/dev/null || printf 0)" = 5 ] || fail "The repository has the wrong number of commits."
[ -n "$(git -C "$repo" status --porcelain)" ] || fail "The expected unfinished change is missing."
expected_staged=$(printf "%s\n" '' | sort)
actual_staged=$(git -C "$repo" diff --cached --name-only | sort)
[ "$actual_staged" = "$expected_staged" ] || fail "The staged file set is not correct."
git -C "$repo" check-ignore -q -- generated/summary.txt || fail 'generated/summary.txt should be ignored.'
git -C "$repo" check-ignore -q -- generated/preview.txt || fail 'generated/preview.txt should be ignored.'
git -C "$repo" check-ignore -q -- debug.log || fail 'debug.log should be ignored.'
[ -f "$repo/contacts.txt" ] || fail 'contacts.txt is missing.'
[ "$(cat "$repo/contacts.txt")" = 'Community contacts

Help desk: 000-0000' ] || fail 'contacts.txt does not contain the required text.'

learningGoals: Restore an unwanted unstaged working-tree edit
Unstage a path without discarding its working copy
Correct a staged accidental rewrite in the safe layer order
Preserve existing commits while correcting current state
